clean_story:
Being without requirement. Time as its stage.

The word existence comes from Latin existere—“to stand out” or “emerge.” Like a shadow only visible when light hits an object, existence isn’t a thing itself but the fact that things are, even briefly.

A rock exists by occupying space, resisting your kick. A thought exists as electrical pulses in your brain, fading like ripples in water. Both demand no permission. Even a single hydrogen atom, drifting between galaxies for 13 billion years, exists—unseen, unmeasured, yet undeniably there.

The universe’s oldest light, the cosmic microwave background, is existence’s receipt: proof that something (a hot, dense point) became everything (stars, dust, you). No instruction manual. No expiration date.

Three related subjects are nothingness before time, how matter resists non-existence, and consciousness as observer.
